author: @MihailoMilankovic
text: Could you show us if you'd paint over the conductive inc (any color really), would it still work? Thanks :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: certainly - though i did over varnish the inks in the two lamp vids - but i think you are right a demo of over painting is a really good idea

author: @WileHeCoyote
text: If i was to build the touch feature into an outside light, would  rain make it flicker or fail? and or is the conductive paint water soluble after drying? i want to paint large rocks and boulders for my switch. thanks for the great video Rob and team FWG!!!    (i also really quite like the old Edison filament style bulbs, but i like the imitation led ones even more. all the steam punk, with 1/8th the energy)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you can over varnish it mate and it still works - but by itself it is non waterproof - I will do a video on over painting

author: @richardsandwell2285
text: Great project Robert, one type of adhesive I use a lot of is modified silicone polymer, you may find it useful.  It comes in sealant gun cartridges I have mended concrete slabs with the stuff, even thrown in dry sand and or made a sticky mortar for pointing up which is flexible for joints.  You can buy it in clear or various colours, it is stronger than silicone sealant and can be painted over.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: do you know what the trade name is mate?

replies.author: @richardsandwell2285
replies.text: Various brands and they vary slightly in quality, you get what you pay for really. C-Tec CT1 is a good one,  Soudal Fix all Clear from Screwfix, I think Evo stick do one and another brand called Tec 7. It will not dry fast like Super Glue, its more like a silicone and so powerfully strong bonds. Usually various colours or a water clear version, but must admit it does not stay clear for long.

author: @ollieb9875
text: I have another question sir I'm sorry! I am keen to perhaps get hold of some of your conductive ink and so but I can't afford it this week so I'll grab a kit in a week or two..  What I wondered about was the touch part of your "transformer" and what's inside! Some kind of latching FET?, .. or a 4011 NAND circuit? Something crazy? It would be wonderful to see one on breadboard or something. I can see kind of how to do it with bits I have, clearly not in a safe way to plug to the mains though I wouldn't do that. USB I could conceivably cobble together with some furious googling yeah!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i'll take a pic and post it mate

replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith really?! Wow thank you sir! This weather is melting my brain. Be well! 😁💚

author: @ericlinz4626
text:
Ahhh...  I get it...  you are beginning to realize the power of effective media and increasing the quality of your videos.  This is wise.  By making your videos of higher quality it will make the battery projects more interesting to learn about.  I remember some of those early videos where the production quality was terrible (sound often nearly impossible to hear) and these newer ones are much better.

I'm still more interested in the battery stuff, but now is a great time to practice new skills.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you are right the first vids were terrible lol - but practice and a decent camera seem to work wonders lol

author: @jamest.5001
text: Gollie! It's already the first of July! Where is the time a going! I'm going to have go clip its wings! To keep the time from flying!!!! Ha-ha. Bad joke! Nice lamp. I went through a lamp making phase! Every thing I saw. I thought of ways to make it a lamp! Trash cans, bird houses, old lanterns, flower pots, strainers. Like used with pasta! All kinds of stuff. Thank goodness I didn't actually make all that stuff! I'm off grid. And working on a DC whole house lighting system. With led lights. With lithium ion backup. I haven't tried yet. But do you know of a circuit I could use to charge the 3s 11.1 nominal volts. And charge the battery. And switch over to battery when power is lost! Do you know of something like that? I need to use the house 12v if power is lost. I need the batt pack to keep the lights on! Nice lamp. AGAIN!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I'm afraid i don't really mate - but i will keep a look out
